title: Souroubea amazonica
taxonomy: Souroubea amazonica is classified within the family Marcgraviaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the clammy apricot or clusioid family. This family is part of the order Theales, a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. Souroubea amazonica, along with other plants in the Marcgraviaceae family, is characterized by their unique inflorescences and epiphytic growth habits. The family Marcgraviaceae is native to the Americas, with the majority of species found in Central and South America. Souroubea amazonica's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the clusioid family.

intro: Souroubea amazonica, also known as "Igarapava" or "Ucuuba amazónica", is a tropical plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the family Marcgraviaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Souroubea amazonica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made with Souroubea amazonica include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
